post.content: Its very disappointing to see good companies expecting candidates to solve the most optimal solution within 45min interview and if they don\'t , direct rejection .\nEven though the truth is known to them that these won\'t be implemented in real world systems (90% time , we just want the feature to be done due to product urgency , very few people care about time complexity and space complexity in real world systems...though some systems do require the less space and time ) ..\nGiven enough time , the candidate will be able to solve the optimal solution with a little bit of help from senior/google /GPT.
The requirements these days are crazy. You either know the solution up front or you fail. "Problem solving" on the spot is not rewarded.
Can anyone tell solution of 2 part. How to do in O(LogN)?
Hey, thanks for sharing! For Question 1. Do you think it\'s this one? - https://leetcode.com/problems/sum-of-absolute-differences-in-a-sorted-array/\nThanks!

can we use binary search to find the range of K numbers such that square of  0th and kth element in the range is smaller than both -1 and n+1 index, if not we can move pointer on left or right based on which side has smaller value

1) Can create a freq array of max  positive integer range available for numbers, and can increase frequency of mod of number, iterate through this freq array again and add to result list as per the freq.\n2) Create a tree set, add all numbers to this set and poll first from set until k > 0, to get kth smallest number
